tools.name: get_object_metadata
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive metadata for a specific HDF5 object
tools.detailed_description: Returns information about type, shape, datatype, chunking, compression filters, and other storage properties. For datasets: shape, dtype, chunks, compression settings, fill value. For groups: member count and names. Essential for understanding object properties before operations like visualization or optimization.
use_cases: Checking current chunk configuration before rechunking

tools.name: rechunk_dataset
tools.description: Modify the chunking layout of an HDF5 dataset
tools.detailed_description: Supports high-level adjustments ('larger', 'smaller', 'half', 'double') or exact chunk dimension specifications. Can also convert chunked datasets to contiguous layout. Creates a NEW file using h5repack (original NOT modified). Call get_object_metadata() first to understand current chunk configuration.
use_cases: Optimizing chunk size for access patterns
Converting between chunked and contiguous layouts
Aligning chunks with data access patterns

tools.name: apply_filter_dataset
tools.description: Apply, modify, or remove compression and filter settings on an HDF5 dataset
tools.detailed_description: Supports gzip, szip, shuffle, fletcher32, nbit, and scaleoffset filters. Creates a NEW file using h5repack (original NOT modified). Call get_object_metadata() first to understand current filter configuration.
use_cases: Compressing datasets to reduce file size
Applying shuffle filter to improve compression

tools.name: visualize
tools.description: Generate a visualization (PNG image) of an HDF5 dataset
tools.detailed_description: Automatically selects appropriate plot types (line, histogram, heatmap, contour, etc.) based on data dimensionality and semantic metadata, or accepts explicit plot type specification. Supports memory-efficient HDF5-level slicing for large datasets, dimension selection, and axis scaling. Designed to work with get_object_metadata() and read_semantic_metadata() to make informed visualization decisions.

tools.name: read_semantic_metadata
tools.description: Read the semantic metadata attribute associated with a given object
tools.detailed_description: Returns the SMD text content if it exists, or an error/info message if the object or its SMD attribute is not found. Acts on a single object only (not recursive).
use_cases: Checking if SMD exists for an object

tools.name: write_semantic_metadata
tools.description: Write or update the semantic metadata attribute for a single HDF5 object
tools.detailed_description: By default, prefixes content with 'BEST GUESS: ' to indicate inferred metadata, though this can be disabled when writing verified ground-truth information. Acts on a single object only (not recursive).

tools.name: collect_objects_for_smd
tools.description: Scan the file structure and collect a batch of objects that lack semantic metadata
tools.detailed_description: Returns up to SMD_GENERATION_BATCH_SIZE objects along with their structural metadata (type, shape, attributes, etc.), enabling an agent to generate appropriate SMD for each. Recursively processes groups. Designed for iterative SMD generation workflows.

tools.name: write_smd_batch
tools.description: Write semantic metadata for multiple objects simultaneously in a single transaction
tools.detailed_description: Designed to work with collect_objects_for_smd() in an iterative workflow for efficiently generating SMD across an entire file. Continues processing even if individual writes fail, returning detailed success/failure information for each object.
use_cases: Writing SMD for multiple objects at once

tools.name: vectorize_semantic_metadata
tools.description: Convert all text-based semantic metadata into searchable vector embeddings
tools.detailed_description: Reads all SMD attributes (ahdf5-smd-*), embeds them using a sentence transformer model, and stores the resulting embeddings in a /ahdf5-vsmd group structure within the file for efficient semantic search. Processes SMD in batches to manage memory usage. Embeddings are L2-normalized to enable cosine similarity searches via dot product.

tools.name: query_semantic_metadata
tools.description: Perform natural language semantic search over vectorized semantic metadata
tools.detailed_description: Takes a query string (e.g., 'temperature in Celsius') and returns the top-k most semantically similar objects from the file based on their SMD. Requires that vectorize_semantic_metadata() has been run first. Supports filtering by path prefix and minimum similarity score thresholds.
use_cases: Finding datasets by natural language query
Discovering relevant data without knowing paths
